recipes.title: Kottu
recipes.description: Chopped roti stir-fried on a hot griddle with vegetables, egg, and meat in a fiery curry sauce.
steps: Cook chicken in a pan with curry powder, salt, and a splash of water until tender; shred.
Heat oil on a flat griddle and saute curry leaves, leeks, carrot, and cabbage briefly.
Push vegetables aside and scramble the eggs.
Add shredded chicken and any cooking liquid.
Stack roti and chop into thin strips with two metal blades.
Add roti to the griddle and chop and fold everything together with rapid hammering motions.
Splash soy sauce and salt to taste, then serve hot.

recipes.title: Hoppers
recipes.description: Bowl-shaped fermented rice flour and coconut milk pancakes with a soft centre and lacy crisp edges.
steps: Whisk warm water with yeast and sugar; rest 10 minutes until frothy.
Mix rice flour, salt, and the yeast mixture into a smooth batter.
Cover and ferment 4 hours in a warm spot until bubbly.
Stir in coconut milk to a thin pancake-like consistency.
Heat a small hopper pan and ladle in batter, swirling immediately to coat the sides.
Cover for 1 minute until edges are crisp and centre is set.
Lift out and serve hot with seeni sambol or pol sambol.

recipes.title: Lamprais
recipes.description: Burgher-Sri Lankan rice bundles with mixed meat curry, frikadeller, and aubergine, baked in banana leaf.
steps: Cook the mixed meat with lamprais curry powder, coconut milk, and salt until tender.
Fry the rice in ghee with whole spices, then cook in the meat broth until just done.
Form minced beef into small frikadeller balls and fry until brown.
Slice and fry aubergine until soft for brinjal moju.
Soften banana leaves over a flame.
Place rice in the centre, top with curry, frikadeller, aubergine, and a smear of shrimp paste.
Fold leaves into a parcel and bake at 180 C for 20 minutes; serve hot in the leaf.

recipes.title: Pol Sambol
recipes.description: Fresh coconut sambol pounded with chili, lime, onion, and Maldive fish — Sri Lanka's iconic relish.
steps: Place grated coconut in a wide bowl.
Pound red onion with chili powder and salt in a mortar until paste-like.
Add the onion paste to the coconut along with maldive fish.
Mix vigorously with your fingertips until the coconut turns rosy.
Squeeze in lime juice and adjust salt.
Serve immediately with rice, hoppers, or string hoppers.

recipes.title: Fish Ambul Thiyal
recipes.description: Sour dry tuna curry preserved with goraka and roasted spices, a classic southern Sri Lankan dish.
steps: Soak goraka in hot water for 15 minutes, then grind to a paste.
Cut tuna into thick chunks and rinse.
Pound garlic, ginger, and pepper in a mortar.
Combine all spices, goraka paste, salt, and a splash of water in a clay pot.
Add fish and toss to coat, then add curry and pandan leaves.
Cover and cook on low heat for 25 minutes, gently shaking the pot occasionally.
The dish is done when liquid has fully evaporated and fish is dark and dry.

recipes.title: Kiribath
recipes.description: Milk rice cooked in coconut milk and pressed into diamonds, a Sri Lankan staple at celebrations.
steps: Wash rice well and cook with water and salt until just tender and water is mostly absorbed.
Pour in thick coconut milk and stir well.
Cook on low heat, stirring constantly, until coconut milk is fully absorbed and the rice is creamy.
Press the cooked rice firmly into a flat tray to about 2 cm thickness.
Smooth the top with a wet spatula and let it cool for 15 minutes.
Cut into diamond shapes with a wet knife.
Serve warm with lunu miris, pol sambol, or jaggery.

recipes.title: String Hoppers
recipes.description: Steamed nests of pressed rice flour noodles, served at breakfast with curries and sambols.
steps: Mix string hopper flour with salt in a wide bowl.
Pour in boiling water gradually, mixing with a spoon to a soft dough.
Knead the dough briefly until smooth.
Pack into a string hopper press.
Press the dough through the press onto greased string hopper mats in tight circular patterns.
Steam in a multi-tier steamer for 8 minutes until set.
Carefully lift off the mats and serve hot with kiri hodi and pol sambol.

recipes.title: Watalappan
recipes.description: Sri Lankan Malay steamed coconut and jaggery custard fragrant with cardamom and nutmeg.
steps: Melt jaggery with 4 tablespoons of water in a saucepan until syrupy; cool.
Whisk eggs lightly in a bowl.
Add coconut milk, jaggery syrup, cardamom, and nutmeg; whisk until smooth.
Strain through a fine sieve into a heatproof bowl.
Cover with foil and steam for 35 minutes until set with a slight wobble.
Cool to room temperature, then refrigerate for at least 2 hours.
Garnish with toasted cashews before serving.

recipes.title: Dhal Curry
recipes.description: Sri Lankan red lentil curry tempered with curry leaves, mustard seeds, and creamy coconut milk.
steps: Wash lentils until water runs clear.
Cook lentils with turmeric, water, and salt until soft and creamy.
Add coconut milk and pandan leaf; simmer 5 minutes more.
In a separate pan, heat coconut oil and pop mustard seeds.
Add curry leaves, sliced onion, garlic, and dried chilies; saute until fragrant.
Pour the tempering over the lentils and stir.
Adjust salt and serve with rice or hoppers.
